title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Fatigue
intro: Herbal teas for fatigue are a type of herbal remedy that involves consuming tea made from various plant extracts to alleviate the symptoms of fatigue, which is a state of physical, mental, and emotional exhaustion. These teas work by providing a natural boost to the body, increasing energy levels, and promoting a sense of well-being. Examples of herbal teas that can treat fatigue include peppermint tea, which helps to improve mental clarity and focus, ginger tea, which aids in digestion and reduces inflammation, chamomile tea, which calms the mind and body, and ginseng tea, which enhances physical and mental energy. Additionally, other herbal teas such as ashwagandha tea, licorice root tea, and rosehip tea have also been known to help alleviate fatigue due to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, making them a popular choice for those seeking a natural remedy.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common symptom of various health conditions, due to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng teas, including ginsenosides, panaxosides, and saponins, help to reduce fatigue by promoting cellular energy production, improving mental clarity, and enhancing physical performance. By stimulating the body's natural response to stress, Panax ginseng teas help to alleviate fatigue and improve overall well-being, making them a popular herbal preparation for individuals seeking a natural remedy. The benefits of Panax ginseng teas in treating fatigue include improved sleep quality, enhanced physical endurance, and increased mental alertness.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Take 2-4 grams of dried Panax ginseng root. You can use 1-2 teaspoons of the root as a substitute.
Add the Panax ginseng root to the boiling water. Reduce heat to a simmer.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. You can strain the tea if you prefer a clearer drink.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink. You can add honey or sugar if you like it sweet.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Biochemical pharmacology", Panax ginseng teas for fatigue may be beneficial due to their potential to enhance nitric oxide synthesis in the endothelium, which could contribute to vasodilatation and possibly alleviate fatigue.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can cause side effects such as insomnia, headaches, and stomach upset when used to treat fatigue, particularly if consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with small doses, monitor body reactions, and avoid combining Panax ginseng teas with other stimulants or medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Panax ginseng tea, 2 times a day.
Start with 300 mg of ginseng extract per serving.
Gradually increase to 400-500 mg as needed.
Use for 2-4 weeks to experience fatigue relief.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by a lack of energy and motivation. The properties of this herbal preparation that help to treat fatigue include its ability to improve blood circulation, reduce oxidative stress, and enhance cognitive function. Ginkgo biloba teas contain bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, which have been shown to help alleviate fatigue by improving mitochondrial function and increasing the production of ATP, the primary energy source in cells. The benefits of Ginkgo biloba teas in treating fatigue include improved mental clarity, increased physical energy, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for those seeking to combat this debilitating condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) and place it in a cup.
Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the cup with the boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into the cup with the honey (if using).
Drink the tea and enjoy. You can drink 1-2 cups per day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Presse medicale (Paris, France : 1983)", Ginkgo biloba teas for fatigue may be beneficial in improving short-term memory, particularly at a dose of 600 mg.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be effective in alleviating fatigue, but some users may experience side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its ability to thin the blood and interact with certain medications. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid taking Ginkgo biloba teas with other medications that thin the blood, and monitor their blood pressure and liver function regularly.
preparation_usage: Start with 1/2 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea, 2-3 times a day.
Drink tea 30 minutes before meals to aid digestion and energy.
Take 250-500 mg of Ginkgo biloba extract daily for optimal fatigue relief.
Monitor your energy levels and adjust dosage as needed, up to 1 cup of tea.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized Ginkgo biloba tea advice.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat fatigue, a common ailment affecting millions worldwide. The relaxing properties of this herbal preparation help to treat fatigue by promoting a sense of calmness and reducing anxiety, allowing individuals to feel more energized and focused. The bioactive constituents of Valeriana officinalis, including valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates, play a crucial role in treating fatigue by acting as a sedative and reducing the activity of neurotransmitters responsible for stress and anxiety. The benefits of Valeriana officinalis teas in treating fatigue include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety levels, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking a holistic approach to managing fatigue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Valeriana officinalis root, 2 cups of water, and a tea infuser.
Rinse the Valeriana root and chop it into small pieces.
Place the chopped root in the tea infuser and put it in a pot.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the Valeriana root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root. Drink the tea hot or cold as needed for fatigue.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Valeriana officinalis teas may be beneficial for fatigue, as part of the herbal medicines showing the most potential for treating anxiety and insomnia, along with passionflower and ashwagandha.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be an effective natural remedy for alleviating fatigue, but its use may be associated with potential side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, particularly those used to treat anxiety, insomnia, or high blood pressure. When using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat fatigue, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding use before or after consuming alcohol, and being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery, to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Valeriana officinalis tea.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root in 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Take 1-2 cups of tea 1-2 times a day, as needed for fatigue.
Continue use for 2-4 weeks, then reassess and adjust dosage if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue ailments due to their adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in the body. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis, including schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides, help to improve physical and mental endurance, reducing fatigue and exhaustion. By enhancing the body's ability to cope with stress and promoting the production of energy-boosting hormones, Schisandra chinensis teas help to alleviate fatigue and improve overall well-being. The benefits of using Schisandra chinensis teas to treat fatigue include improved mental clarity, increased energy levels, and enhanced physical performance, making them a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking to manage fatigue and improve their quality of life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Schisandra chinensis, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure and add 1 teaspoon of dried Schisandra chinensis to the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Schisandra chinensis in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Drink the Schisandra chinensis tea warm or at room temperature, 1-2 times a day for fatigue relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", Schisandra chinensis teas for fatigue may effectively increase mental performance and physical working capacity in humans, with minimal side effects, due to its single dose stimulating effects associated with the sympatho-adrenal-system (SAS).
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can be an effective treatment for fatigue, but its use may lead to potential side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its high content of glycosides and volatile oils. When using Schisandra chinensis teas to treat fatigue, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it with other stimulants or medications, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to the risk of drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor or herbalist for proper dosage and guidance.
Start with 2-3 teaspoons of Schisandra chinensis tea per cup, 1-2 times a day.
Begin with a low dose and gradually increase as needed and under guidance.
Consume Schisandra chinensis tea for 2-3 weeks to assess its effect on fatigue.
Continue as needed and adjust dosage under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Withania somnifera teas, also known as ashwagandha, are a traditional herbal preparation used to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by physical, mental, or emotional exhaustion. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat fatigue by reducing stress, anxiety, and inflammation, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Withania somnifera teas, including withanolides and alkaloids, have been shown to have adaptogenic properties, helping to regulate cortisol levels and increase energy levels. By incorporating Withania somnifera teas into their daily routine, individuals can benefit from improved physical and mental resilience, enhanced cognitive function, and a reduced risk of fatigue-related disorders.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 tablespoon of Withania somnifera powder or dried roots.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of powder or roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it when it's warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "International journal of Ayurveda research", Withania somnifera teas for fatigue may be beneficial as they increased velocity, power and VO2 max, which suggests improved endurance and energy levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Withania somnifera teas can be used to treat fatigue, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of withanolides, which can interact with certain medications. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, drink plenty of water, and avoid consuming the teas on an empty stomach, especially when taking other medications or supplements.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Withania somnifera in 1 cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Continue using for up to 3 months for optimal fatigue relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by physical and mental exhaustion. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin, a bioactive constituent of Glycyrrhiza glabra, help to reduce oxidative stress and promote overall well-being. Glycyrrhizin, along with other bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, also exhibit adaptogenic properties that help to regulate the body's response to stress, thereby alleviating fatigue. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been found to provide relief from fatigue by promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and boosting energy levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage fatigue.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pharmacology, biochemistry, and behavior", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for fatigue may have antifatigue properties as it was found to increase glucose levels and decrease lactate dehydrogenase and blood urea nitrogen levels in mice.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to alleviate fatigue, but its prolonged use may lead to potential side effects such as weight gain, high blood pressure, and changes in potassium levels. To safely use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for fatigue relief, it is recommended to limit the duration of use, monitor blood pressure and potassium levels, and avoid consuming excessive amounts of tea to prevent overexposure to glycyrrhizin.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried roots per cup of boiling water.
Take 200-300 mg of Glycyrrhizin per serving, 2-3 times a day.
Start with low dose and gradually increase as needed.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage and guidance.

preparation_amazon.title: Pukka Herbal Teas Licorice and Cinnamon - 20 Bags, 20 Count

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue due to their calming and relaxing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat fatigue by promoting a good night's sleep, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving mood. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, thereby alleviating fatigue. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat fatigue include improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and increased energy levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it reaches a boil.
Remove the pot from heat and add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the flowers from the water.
Drink the tea hot or cold, up to 3 times a day as needed for fatigue relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat fatigue, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using these teas to alleviate fatigue, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which may lead to interactions with medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions, and to monitor for any adverse reactions, especially in people with sensitive stomachs or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water
Adjust dosage to 2 teaspoons if fatigue persists after 3 days
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable fatigue relief
Consult a healthcare professional for prolonged fatigue or underlying issues

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common condition characterized by persistent tiredness and lack of energy. The herbal preparation's properties, including its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory effects, help to alleviate fatigue by regulating the body's stress response and improving its ability to cope with physical and mental demands. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus teas, such as astragalosides, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, which include enhancing cellular energy production, improving mitochondrial function, and promoting antioxidant defenses. Regular consumption of Astragalus membranaceus teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating fatigue, including improved physical performance, enhanced mental clarity, and increased overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Astragalus membranaceus root for every cup of water.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot, then reduce heat to medium.
Add the measured Astragalus membranaceus root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it hot or let it cool down before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Complementary therapies in medicine", Astragalus membranaceus teas for fatigue may have a pharmacological effect, as a study showed that administration of a Myelophil extract, containing Astragalus membranaceus, significantly decreased the fatigue severity score in adults with chronic fatigue.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses, gradually increase as needed, and be cautious with interactions, especially with medications, and avoid consumption during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Astragalus per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Continue usage for 4-6 weeks to manage fatigue effectively.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosing and guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat fatigue and promote relaxation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to fatigue. The bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid and apigenin, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to regulate sleep patterns and alleviate fatigue. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat fatigue include improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and increased energy levels, making it a popular herbal remedy for individuals looking to manage fatigue and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help alleviate fatigue.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC complementary medicine and therapies", Melissa officinalis teas for fatigue may not be directly addressed as the study primarily focused on the anti-depression and anti-anxiety effects of Melissa officinalis in patients with type 2 diabetes, but based on the study, it can be inferred that consuming Melissa officinalis may potentially help reduce fatigue by decreasing depression and anxiety severity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be effective in treating fatigue, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Melissa officinalis teas in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day, and to avoid taking it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional for dosage and guidance on using Melissa officinalis tea.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Drink 1-3 cups of Melissa officinalis tea per day, 2-3 times a week.
Monitor fatigue levels and adjust dosage as directed by your healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by physical, mental, or emotional exhaustion. The herbal preparation's adaptogenic properties help to treat fatigue by regulating the body's response to stress, promoting balance, and enhancing overall well-being. The bioactive constituents, including catechins, theaflavins, and thearubigins, in Camellia sinensis teas help to treat fatigue by increasing energy levels, improving mood, and reducing inflammation. The benefits of consuming Camellia sinensis teas to treat fatigue include improved physical performance, enhanced mental clarity, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases associated with fatigue.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Measure 1 teaspoon of Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or white) and place in a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the tea leaves and let steep for 1-3 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the tea to help reduce fatigue. Repeat as needed, up to 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to The American journal of Chinese medicine, Camellia sinensis teas for fatigue, as part of commercial Chinese herbal teas, are used to restore strength.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat fatigue, but consuming them excessively may lead to side effects such as insomnia, digestive issues, and rapid heartbeat due to their high caffeine content. To use Camellia sinensis teas safely, it is essential to brew them according to the recommended guidelines, start with small amounts, and avoid mixing them with other stimulants or medications to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat fatigue due to their stimulating properties that help to increase alertness and vitality. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, present in these teas have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to their fatigue-fighting properties. By promoting blood circulation, reducing oxidative stress, and alleviating muscle pain, Zingiber officinale teas help to alleviate fatigue and improve overall well-being. Regular consumption of these teas has been associated with various benefits, including enhanced mental clarity, improved physical performance, and a reduced risk of chronic fatigue syndrome.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Measure 1/2 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root powder into a tea strainer or infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the root powder in the strainer or infuser.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the strainer or infuser.
Drink the tea hot or let it cool, and enjoy as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Zingiber officinale teas, as part of a fortified tea, significantly improved the natural killer (NK) cell activity of volunteers, which can help boost the immune system and potentially alleviate fatigue.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat fatigue, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content, which can be irritating to the digestive system. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and drink plenty of water to help alleviate potential side effects like stomach discomfort and bloating.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a condition characterized by a feeling of weariness, lack of energy, and a decreased ability to perform daily activities. The anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties of Echinacea purpurea teas help to treat fatigue by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, thereby alleviating fatigue symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and flavonoids, play a crucial role in treating fatigue by protecting against oxidative stress, modulating cytokine production, and enhancing immune function. Drinking Echinacea purpurea teas may help to reduce fatigue and improve overall well-being due to its adaptogenic properties, which help the body to adapt to stress and promote energy balance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers. Use a ratio of 1 cup of flowers for every 2 cups of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea root (optional) and add it to the flowers. If using fresh flowers, skip this step.
Combine the Echinacea flowers and root (if using) in a heat-resistant cup or teapot. Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the mixture.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes. If using fresh flowers, steep for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth. Discard the solids and enjoy your Echinacea purpurea tea for fatigue relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica, Echinacea purpurea teas may be used for fatigue due to its effects in invigorating Qi, strengthening body resistance, and treating body deficiency and multiple diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, take them in moderation, ideally after meals, and avoid combining them with other stimulants or medications that may interact with their active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to alleviate fatigue due to their adaptogenic properties. The bioactive constituents, including carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor, in these teas help to promote antioxidant activity, reduce inflammation, and improve mental clarity, thus contributing to the treatment of fatigue. By stimulating the mind and body, Rosmarinus officinalis teas help to increase energy levels, enhance focus, and improve overall well-being, making them a beneficial herbal preparation for managing fatigue. The benefits of consuming Rosmarinus officinalis teas for fatigue include reduced oxidative stress, improved mental performance, and a sense of rejuvenation and revitalization.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis (Rosemary) leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove until it boils.
Pour the boiling water over the rosemary leaves in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.
Drink the rosemary tea while it's still warm, ideally 1-2 cups per day for fatigue relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat fatigue, but potential side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high concentration of volatile oils. To minimize risks, it is recommended to consume Rosmarinus officinalis teas in moderation, ideally after meals, and to avoid taking it with other stimulants or medications that can interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Rhodiola rosea teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by physical, mental, and emotional exhaustion. The herbal preparation's adaptogenic properties help to treat fatigue by promoting balance in the body's physiological response to stress, thereby reducing the negative effects of fatigue. The bioactive constituents of Rhodiola rosea teas, including rosavin, rosin, and salidroside, help to treat fatigue by enhancing mental performance, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the body's response to cortisol. The benefits of using Rhodiola rosea teas to treat fatigue include improved mood, increased energy levels, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking a holistic approach to managing fatigue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Rhodiola rosea root, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Place the dried Rhodiola rosea root in the tea infuser or directly into the heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Rhodiola rosea root, making sure it's fully covered.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the Rhodiola rosea root from the water.
Strain the tea into another cup, if needed, and drink immediately while it's still warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Rhodiola rosea teas for fatigue may be beneficial in reducing stress-induced fatigue and enhancing physical performance.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rhodiola rosea teas can cause stomach upset, headaches, and dizziness in some individuals when used to treat fatigue, and may also interact with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and be cautious when consuming Rhodiola rosea teas with other stimulants or sedatives to avoid exacerbating fatigue or other health issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been used for centuries to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by physical, mental, and emotional exhaustion. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, help to alleviate fatigue by promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and enhancing energy levels. The bioactive constituents of Centella asiatica teas, such as asiatic acid and madecassoside, have been shown to have anti-fatigue effects by improving sleep quality, reducing stress, and enhancing cognitive function. Regular consumption of Centella asiatica teas can provide numerous benefits, including improved mood, increased energy, and enhanced physical performance, making it a popular natural remedy for fatigue relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Centella asiatica leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Centella asiatica leaves to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1 cup of the Centella asiatica tea, 2-3 times a day, to help with fatigue.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytomedicine: international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, Centella asiatica teas for fatigue have been found to improve fatigue in elderly patients, as indicated by a significant improvement in the physical component summary of the SF-36 analysis in a study on Hochuekkito, which contains Centella asiatica as one of its components.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be beneficial in treating fatigue, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its potential to interact with certain medications or exacerbate existing gastrointestinal conditions. Precautions include using the tea in moderation, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and not combining it with other stimulants or medications, as this may worsen symptoms or lead to adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Aspalathus linearis teas, also known as rooibos tea, have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, particularly in individuals experiencing chronic exhaustion. The properties of this herbal preparation, such as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, help to treat fatigue by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Rooibos tea contains bioactive constituents like aspalathin, nothofagin, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects against fatigue by improving energy metabolism, enhancing cognitive function, and promoting relaxation. The benefits of consuming Aspalathus linearis teas for fatigue treatment include improved energy levels, enhanced mental clarity, and a reduced risk of chronic fatigue syndrome.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aspalathus linearis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help manage fatigue
preparations.preparation_study: According to Frontiers in pharmacology, Aspalathus linearis teas are not mentioned in the given scientific study for fatigue, but the study discusses the efficacy of Japanese herbal preparations, such as Hochuekkito, Juzentaihoto, and Rikkunshito, which contain multi-component extracts of medicinal plants, in improving fatigue and cachexia symptoms in various patient populations.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aspalathus linearis teas can be used to treat fatigue, but it may cause side effects such as headaches, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large amounts. To use Aspalathus linearis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid mixing it with other stimulants or medications that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by physical, mental, and emotional exhaustion. The herbal preparation's sedative and anxiolytic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to fatigue, promoting relaxation and calmness. The bioactive constituents of Passiflora incarnata, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, such as flavogallonone, flavogallonone glycoside, and harmine, play a crucial role in mitigating fatigue by modulating neurotransmitter activity and improving sleep quality. The benefits of Passiflora incarnata teas in treating fatigue include improved sleep, reduced stress and anxiety, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking a holistic approach to managing fatigue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata leaves. If using fresh leaves, choose healthy ones with no signs of mold or pests.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the Passiflora incarnata leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in the tea infuser or cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup to remove the leaves. Discard the leaves and reserve the tea.
Drink the Passiflora incarnata tea as needed to help alleviate fatigue. You can have 2-3 cups per day, but consult a doctor before making it a regular habit.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of toxicology, clinical toxicology, Passiflora incarnata teas for fatigue may cause significant adverse effects, including severe nausea, vomiting, drowsiness, prolonged QTc, and episodes of nonsustained ventricular tachycardia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating fatigue, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use this herbal preparation safely, one should start with a low dose, be cautious of interactions with other medications, and avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, also known as oat straw tea, are a natural remedy used to treat fatigue and related symptoms. This herbal preparation contains a compound called avenacoside, which has a sedative effect and helps to calm the nervous system, contributing to its fatigue-fighting properties. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa teas, including avenanthramides and avenacosides, have been shown to improve sleep quality, reduce stress, and increase energy levels by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas has been found to provide relief from fatigue and improve overall well-being by addressing the root causes of the ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Avena sativa in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Avena sativa and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the Avena sativa tea warm or at room temperature, ideally before bedtime.
preparations.preparation_study: According to International journal of gynaecology and obstetrics: the official organ of the International Federation of Gynaecology and Obstetrics, Avena sativa teas for fatigue may not be directly mentioned in this study, but the study on Korean red ginseng suggests that a decrease in cortisol/DHEA-S ratio, which is associated with reduced stress levels, may contribute to improvement in fatigue among postmenopausal women.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be beneficial in treating fatigue, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties and potential interactions with other medications. To minimize side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming Avena sativa teas with other sedatives or stimulants, and maintain a consistent sleep schedule to reduce the risk of adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Eleutherococcus senticosus teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common health issue characterized by persistent tiredness and lack of energy. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, help to treat fatigue by enhancing the body's ability to resist stress and promote overall well-being. Bioactive constituents such as eleutherosides, particularly eleutheroside E, play a crucial role in treating fatigue by modulating the body's physiological response to stress, improving endurance, and reducing oxidative stress. The benefits of Eleutherococcus senticosus teas in treating fatigue include improved physical performance, enhanced mental clarity, and a reduced risk of fatigue-related illnesses, making it a popular natural remedy among athletes and individuals with high-stress lifestyles.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Eleutherococcus senticosus root, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the dried root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the root, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root, then add honey or lemon to taste if desired.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help manage fatigue, and as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Eleutherococcus senticosus teas have been found to have anti-fatigue effects, which are part of its overall pharmacological effect on treating fatigue.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eleutherococcus senticosus teas can cause stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To use Eleutherococcus senticosus teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid taking it with other stimulants, and be cautious of potential interactions with medications, and to drink plenty of water to help reduce the risk of side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas have been traditionally used to treat fatigue, a common ailment characterized by physical and mental exhaustion. The herbal preparation contains various bioactive constituents, including alkaloids such as reserpine, serpentinine, and yohimbine, which have been shown to possess sedative, antispasmodic, and vasodilatory properties that help to alleviate fatigue symptoms. These properties help to regulate the body's physiological response to stress, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, thereby reducing feelings of fatigue and exhaustion. The benefits of Rauvolfia serpentina teas in treating fatigue include improved sleep patterns, reduced stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular herbal remedy for individuals seeking natural solutions to this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Rauvolfia serpentina root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the root.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed. Stir well.
Drink the tea once or twice a day as needed to help with fatigue.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rauvolfia serpentina teas can be used to treat fatigue, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and changes in heart rate due to the presence of reserpine, a compound that affects blood pressure and heart function. When using Rauvolfia serpentina teas to treat fatigue, precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, as it may lead to accumulation of reserpine in the body and exacerbate underlying conditions such as heart disease or hypotension.

preparation_study: According to Sports medicine (Auckland, N.Z.), Panax ginseng teas for fatigue may be ineffective in improving or prolonging performance in fatigued humans due to the lack of controlled research and methodological problems in existing studies.
intro_study: According to the study, teas for fatigue may be beneficial due to the presence of bioactive components such as L-theanine, which can help regulate the intracellular second messenger levels, and catechins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory effects.
